channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 🤔 What Really Happens When You Run kubectl apply? Episode 3
item.description: Follow the complete journey of a Kubernetes Deployment—from your terminal to a running container inside the cluster. Most Kubernetes tutorials teach us how to create resources: kubectl apply -f...

item.title: Build a Basic AI Agent From Scratch: Security II
item.description: Previous parts of Build a Basic AI Agent From Scratch: Basic Agent Tools Long Task Planning Human in the Loop & Security You can find and clone this code in this blog series' Github repo. In the...

item.title: Introducing Agent-Up: run multiple agent-built versions of your web app at once
item.description: Agent-Up is an open-source, cross-platform desktop app for running and reviewing multiple AI coding agent workspaces on the same machine. It is built specifically for web application development,...

item.title: Ruby shipped the fix for SleeperGem 45 days before it happened
item.description: On June 3rd, Bundler 4.0.13 shipped a feature called cooldown. The release post described the problem it solves like this: "an account is compromised, a malicious version ships, and any bundle...

item.title: Show HN: I built a command palette for the terminal – 6.2MB, pure Go, no fzf
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/matheuzgomes/decoreba Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48995333 Points: 11 # Comments: 2

item.title: It's OK to Get Lucky
item.description: I couldn't write this post. I'd tried three or four times. I knew there was something I needed to say about feedback and track records and the way both of those quietly poison each other, but...

item.title: Nearly every Kindle is steeply discounted at Best Buy
item.description: If you’ve been thinking about picking up a Kindle before school starts, or for your next vacation, now’s a good time to do it. As part of its Black Friday in July sale, Best Buy has brought the...

item.title: I read the 17-comment Reddit fight about trying Kimi K3 and the answer is way less exciting than people want
item.description: The easiest way to try Kimi K3 right now is Moonshot’s own OpenAI-compatible API, not local inference. That was the real answer in a 17-comment r/openclaw thread about a deceptively simple...

item.title: Apple’s rumored ‘Upgrade’ program brings lease-to-own pricing for iPhones, Macs, and iPads
item.description: As component and RAM shortages drive prices higher, Apple is reportedly launching an "Apple Upgrade" program for leasing new devices. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, it will work similar to...

item.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
item.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

item.title: Show DEV: I built a free macOS app to control Spotify Web player directly from the Notch
item.description: Hey DEV community! 👋 Like many of you, I listen to music while coding all day. However, I got tired of constantly switching browser tabs just to skip a track or see what song is currently playing...

item.title: Meta's AI Models Are Powering the First Wave of Genesis Mission Projects
item.description: Article URL: https://ai.meta.com/blog/genesis-mission-lawrence-berkeley-national-laboratory-segment-anything-dino/?_fb_noscript=1 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48995074...

item.title: Além da Teoria: Implementando WCAG no Angular Moderno (v21+)
item.description: Acessibilidade web (WCAG) é o tipo de tema que todo mundo concorda que é crucial, mas que raramente recebe a atenção prática que merece durante a nossa formação. Seja na faculdade ou na grande...

item.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
item.description: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

item.title: Twitch will let parents stop their teens going live
item.description: Twitch is giving parents more control over how their children are using the streaming platform, including the ability to block them from broadcasting entirely. Parental controls are now available...

item.title: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
item.description: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by...

item.title: Instagram will let users endlessly swap the audio on old posts
item.description: There's a symbiotic - and sometimes frustrating - relationship between social media sites and the creators that depend on them. Platforms need influencers' and creators' content to keep consumers...

item.title: Tesla Robotaxis go to Florida
item.description: It must be earnings day, because Tesla is making a Robotaxi announcement. The company said in a post on X that it was launching its Robotaxi service in Orlando and Tampa, attaching a pair of maps...

item.title: A Sneaky Hacking Tool Targeting AI Infrastructure Is Lurking in Victims’ Blind Spots
item.description: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

item.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
item.description: Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to...

item.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

item.title: Samsung can’t afford to play it safe with Apple’s first foldable looming
item.description: Tomorrow's foldable-centric Galaxy Unpacked event looks like it will be Samsung's biggest in years. The company is expected to launch new versions of its Fold and Flip phones while also...

item.title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe
item.description: "Current economic conditions" have shifted TreeSize's business model.

item.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities
item.description: Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch...

item.title: Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
item.description: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.

item.title: The Light Flip is a minimalist flip phone with a point to prove
item.description: A few years ago, the team at Light shipped the most smartphone-like device they'd ever created. The Light Phone III still kept to its minimalist, no-distractions ethos, but it was a phone-size...

item.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
item.description: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash...

item.title: Critical SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-50522 Under Active Exploitation After Public PoC
item.description: A third SharePoint Server flaw patched by Microsoft as part of its Patch Tuesday update for July 2026 has come under active exploitation, per watchTowr. The vulnerability in question is...

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description: Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments....

item.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
item.description: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.
